-Description: 
-A Java Spectrum-Based Fault Localization (SBFL) desktop app that ranks methods by suspicion score using test coverage data.
+A Java desktop app that ranks methods by suspicion score using spectrum-based fault localization (SBFL). It reads test coverage data, computes scores using your chosen formula, and displays results ranked from most to least suspicious with color-coded rows.
 
-## Requirements
 
-- Java 17+
-- Maven 3.6+
+REQUIREMENTS
 
-Verify:
+    Java 17 or higher
+    Maven 3.6 or higher
 
-java -version
-mvn -version
+Verify with:
+    java -version
+    mvn -version
 
-## Launch App
-mvn javafx:run
 
+HOW TO RUN
+
+    mvn javafx:run
+
+
+USING THE APP
+
+1. Click Load Data to load the CoverageData folder automatically.
+   The status bar will show how many tests, methods, and failures were found.
+
+2. Select a formula from the dropdown: Tarantula, Ochiai, Jaccard, or Zoltar.
+
+3. Click Run SBFL to rank all methods by suspicion score.
+   Results appear in the table ranked highest to lowest.
+   Red rows are more suspicious. Green rows are less suspicious.
+
+4. The overview cards at the top show a summary of the loaded dataset.
+   Hover over a card for more detail.
+
+5. The Log section at the bottom can be expanded to see a detailed
+   record of each action including top suspect and score.
+

commits.name: Commit-0
commits.content:
# SBFL JavaFX Project

Description: 
A Java Spectrum-Based Fault Localization (SBFL) desktop app that ranks methods by suspicion score using test coverage data.

## Requirements

- Java 17+
- Maven 3.6+

Verify:

java -version
mvn -version

## Running the App
mvn javafx:run


## Using the Tool

1. **Load Data** — click the button and select a folder containing `.txt` coverage files.
   - Each file represents one test case.
   - Format: first line is `<test_name> true|false` (true = passed, false = failed), followed by one method signature per line.
   - The `CoverageData/` folder in this repo contains sample data.

2. **Select a Formula** — choose from the dropdown:
   - `Tarantula`
   - `Ochiai`
   - `Jaccard`
   - `Zoltar`

3. **Run SBFL** — click the button to rank all methods by suspicion score. Higher scores indicate more likely fault locations.

## Project Structure

```
src/main/java/
├── app/MainApp.java          # JavaFX GUI
├── model/CoverageData.java   # Data model
├── model/Result.java         # Result (method + score)
└── sbfl/
    ├── CoverageParser.java   # Parses .txt coverage files
    ├── Formula.java          # Suspicion formula enum
    └── SBFL.java             # Core ranking logic
CoverageData/                 # Sample coverage files
```
